reasoning: The control '3.4.9 Control and monitor user-installed software' is about ensuring that software installations are controlled and monitored. In a Kubernetes environment, this can be achieved by using admission controllers to enforce software whitelisting policies, ensuring that only approved software can be deployed. Additionally, enabling audit logging for software installations can help monitor changes. Pod Security Policies (or Pod Security Standards) can be used to restrict the installation of unauthorized software by limiting capabilities and enforcing security contexts.
title: 3_4_9: 3.4.9 Control and monitor user-installed software
